stories.title: Leclerc Wins Chaotic British Grand Prix — Ferrari 1-3 at Silverstone

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
LECLERC WINS THE BRITISH GRAND PRIX 🏁

Ferrari 1-3 at Silverstone: Leclerc P1, Russell P2, Hamilton P3.

Antonelli: mechanical failure → demoted to P16.
Verstappen: crashed at Stowe → Safety Car.

The championship is ALIVE. #F1 #BritishGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The full British GP story:

Antonelli ran on pole, looked like a winner with fresh tyres — then his front-left wheel shield failed. Twice into the pits. P16 at the end.

Leclerc, who passed him at the start, never looked back. 9th career win. First at Silverstone. #F1 #BritishGP

article.headline: Leclerc Wins Chaotic British Grand Prix as Antonelli Fails and Verstappen Crashes

research.facts:
Leclerc Wins British Grand Prix — Ferrari 1-3
**Source 1 — Formula1.com official race report:**
- Charles Leclerc wins British Grand Prix from George Russell and Lewis Hamilton
- Antonelli suffered front-left wheel shield failure → twice into pits → finished 9th on road
- Antonelli demoted to 16th after 5-second penalty for track limits violations
- Verstappen crash Lap 48 at Stowe (rear wing fault) → Safety Car
- Race finished under Safety Car
**Source 2 — Sky Sports / Planet F1:**
- Full top 10: Leclerc, Russell, Hamilton, Norris, Hadjar, Lawson, Lindblad, Bortoleto, Colapinto, Gasly
- Hamilton received reprimand (not penalty) for yellow flag infringement → kept P3
- Leclerc's 9th career win, first at Silverstone
- Ferrari 1-3 result
**Source 3 — ESPN / The Race:**
- Antonelli started on pole (from sprint weekend)
- Both Ferraris passed Antonelli at the start
- Antonelli was closing on Leclerc with fresher tyres before wheel shield failure
- "Bizarre" mechanical issue for championship leader
**Confidence:** HIGH (race result confirmed across multiple F1 official/major outlets)

stories.title: Antonelli's Title Lead Cut to 25 Points — Championship Standings Tighten

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
#F1 championship after the British GP:

🥇 Antonelli — 179 pts
🥈 Russell — 154 pts (-25)
🥉 Hamilton — 147 pts (-32)
4️⃣ Leclerc — 108 pts

Antonelli had a 66-point lead after Monaco. It's now 25. Russell is coming. #BritishGP

research.facts:
Championship Standings — Antonelli Lead Cut to 25 Points
**Source 1 — RacingNews365 / Crash.net:**
- Antonelli: 179 points
- Russell: 154 points (gap: 25 points)
- Hamilton: 147 points (gap from leader: 32 points)
- Leclerc: 108 points (overtook Norris to 4th)
**Source 2 — Racing News365 / The Race:**
- Antonelli had built 66-point lead after Monaco GP (5th consecutive win)
- "Two retirements in three races" — Antonelli form has collapsed from Monaco peak
- Russell closed from 66 points down to 25 points since Monaco
- Belgian GP next: July 17-19 at Spa-Francorchamps
**Confidence:** HIGH (points from multiple verified standings sources post-race)

stories.title: Verstappen "Fed Up" with Red Bull After Another Dangerous Crash

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen after crashing at Silverstone due to rear wing failure — again:

"I was lucky in Austria, I was lucky here, but that's why you get really fed up with it."

Red Bull has a dangerous car. The reigning champion is running out of patience. #F1 #RedBull

article.headline: Verstappen "Fed Up" After Second Rear Wing Crash in a Week: "I Don't Want Anything to Do with It for a While"

research.facts:
Verstappen "Fed Up" with Red Bull
**Source 1 — Sky Sports / Motorsport.com / PlanetF1:**
- Verstappen crashed at Stowe on Lap 48 — rear wing not fully closing after opening
- Same issue as Austrian GP qualifying crash "last Saturday" (per Sky)
- Verstappen quote: "I was lucky in Austria, I was lucky here, but that's why you get really fed up with it"
- Said Red Bull dealing with "far too many problems in 2026" — power unit, chassis, rear wing
- Verstappen: "I don't want anything to do with it for a while" (when asked about conversation with management before Belgium)
**Source 2 — Motorsport.com:**
- Verstappen describing the Austrian and British crashes as similar incidents (rear wing DRS not closing)
- Calls it a "dangerous" situation
**Confidence:** HIGH (multiple direct quotes confirmed across Sky Sports, Motorsport.com)

stories.title: Belgian Grand Prix Preview — Spa Awaits After Summer Shock at Silverstone

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The drama at Silverstone is done. Now the calendar turns to Spa.

Belgian Grand Prix: July 17-19 at Spa-Francorchamps.

Antonelli needs a rebound. Leclerc needs to capitalise on momentum. Verstappen needs a safe car.

Two weeks until it all continues. #F1 #BelgianGP

research.facts:
Belgian Grand Prix Preview
**Source 1 — F1 Official Calendar / Sky Sports:**
- Belgian Grand Prix: July 17-19 at Spa-Francorchamps
- Two-week break before Belgium (weekend off from British GP July 5)
- Next round of the 2026 championship season
**Source 2 — Context from race results:**
- Ferrari will arrive in Belgium on momentum (Leclerc win + Hamilton podium)
- Antonelli needs to arrest title lead decline (was 66pts, now 25pts)
- Russell has closed 41 points in three races since Monaco
- Verstappen's future Red Bull issues will be focus of off-week conversation
**Confidence:** MEDIUM-HIGH (dates from official calendar, context inferred from race result trends)

stories.title: Ferrari's Silverstone Statement — Leclerc Win + Hamilton Third Puts Pressure on Mercedes

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Charles Leclerc won the British Grand Prix.
Lewis Hamilton finished on the podium at his home race as a Ferrari driver.

Ferrari 1-3 at Silverstone. +22 points on Mercedes in the constructors' fight.

That's a statement. 🔴 #Ferrari #F1 #BritishGP

research.facts:
Ferrari's Silverstone Statement
**Source 1 — Multiple outlets:**
- Ferrari gains 22 points on Mercedes in constructors' standings after 1-3 result
- Leclerc first win at Silverstone (9th career win total)
- Hamilton's first podium at Ferrari — his home race (British)
- Ferrari's car strength shown by passing Antonelli at start and holding off throughout
**Source 2 — ESPN / BeIN Sports:**
- Hamilton received reprimand not penalty for yellow flag infringement
- Hamilton kept third place after stewards' review
- Both Ferrari drivers on podium in front of packed Silverstone crowd
**Confidence:** HIGH (multiple sources confirm Ferrari 1-3, Hamilton reprimand, points gap to Mercedes)
